title: Peter Hedstrom and Peter Bearman (Eds.): The Oxford Handbook of Analytical Sociology
abstract: ‘Analytical sociology’ is a social scientific research programme whose main purpose is to offer better explanations of social phenomena. Oxford sociologist Peter Hedström, among others, has developed this research programme since the 1990s [Peter Hedström, Richard Swedberg (Eds.) (1998). Social Mechanisms: An Analytical Approach to Social Theory. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 352 pp.; Peter Hedström (2005). Dissecting the Social. On the Principles of Analytical Sociology. Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 177 pp.]. The Oxford Handbook of Analytical Sociology offers a comprehensive overview of the accomplishments of this research programme and provides a research agenda. Although it is not possible to do justice to each of the 30 chapters of the book, in this review I wish to emphasize some of the key analytical concepts and explanatory models that the book contains. I do not review the final chapters on perspectives from fields other than sociology, although these chapters, notably those on game theory and behavioural economics, are very rich and useful.
